old_value: {"id":"broadside-d74n.9","title":"No Content-Length guard on remote fetches","description":"Unbounded resp.bytes().await on actor/WebFinger fetches allows a malicious remote to send multi-gigabyte responses. Must check Content-Length against a cap (e.g. 1MB) before reading. Audit all outbound fetch call sites.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"mark@reviewcommit.com","created_at":"2026-07-18T22:39:33.670145937Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-18T22:39:33.670145937Z","labels":["smallhold-audit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"broadside-d74n.9","depends_on_id":"broadside-d74n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-18T22:39:33.691819581Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value: {"notes":"CONFIRMED: 4 call sites buffer .bytes().await before post-hoc size check. Sites: actor_cache.rs:70, server.rs:863, feed.rs:43, media.rs:80. Fix requires streaming reader with byte cap (resp.bytes_stream() with accumulation limit). Content-Length pre-checks exist but are advisory only (server can omit/lie)."}

Fixed: new src/http.rs read_body_limited() streams chunks via resp.chunk() with hard byte cap. Aborts immediately when limit exceeded — never allocates unbounded memory. Updated all 4 call sites: actor_cache.rs, server.rs (Follow handler), feed.rs, media.rs. All 13 tests passing.
